TextENGNew: ``See you later, Keiichi-kun~!``
With a swift little flourish, she spun on her heel and started walking toward her house.
Hey now, since when did she start imitating Mion?
After walking a little further, she even started skipping along, humming a little tune.
changeReason: The original says 'we had been walking at a swift pace' but Rena had already split off; also ランランラ～ン (la-la-la) indicates she was humming or singing, which the original omits.

TextENGNew: ``Don't trip on anything weird, Rena!``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS17/02/110200054. I'll be totally fine~!``
That's not even a real answer...
Club activities had ended, and as usual, Rena and I walked part of the way home together.
changeReason: The original says they 'walked home from school together' implying all the way; the Japanese 途中まで specifies 'part of the way,' which is important since they live in different directions.

TextENGNew: Rena was in particularly high spirits today.
Apparently it was because she'd claimed first place in a deduction game for the first time ever, but even so, she was practically having a one-woman celebration.
That beaming smile on her face when she handed Mion the shopping list...
Years of pent-up resentment... at last, I shall have my revenge... if you'd be so kind... heh heh heh...
changeReason: The original renders よろしく as 'Thanks~~' but in this dramatic kabuki-style context it means 'if you'd be so kind' (an ominous pleasantry); the original also misattributes the scene to Rena saying something rather than Keiichi's dramatic inner narration.

TextENGNew: ...That was the vibe, anyway.
While I was busy thinking about such trivial things, I arrived home.
I reached for the doorknob and... huh? It won't open?
...Oh, right. Nobody's home.@k Today, Mom went out with Dad for something work-related.
I fished out the key with the fur seal keychain from my pocket to unlock the front door.
I slid it into the keyhole, gave it a turn, and pushed the door open.
The empty house was deathly quiet, and it felt somehow lonely.
I can see why they say an uninhabited house will rot away in no time. It really does feel different without people in it.
changeReason: The original conflates temporary absence with permanent abandonment; the Japanese 人の住まなくなった家 refers to houses where people no longer live, and 立ち腐る means physical rot/decay, not just feeling abandoned.

TextENGNew: I kicked off my shoes and headed upstairs.
I dropped my bag in my room and thought, well... what should I do... As I flopped onto the bed, my stomach let out a loud growl.
...I need to find something to tide me over until dinner.
I went back downstairs to the kitchen and started rummaging through the fridge, the cupboards, anything I could find.
No matter where I looked, I couldn't find even a trace of food.
My shoulders slumped, and I let out a deep, heavy sigh.
I mean, come on... don't parents normally leave something prepared when they go out?
I'd complained about this before, but Mom just brushed me off with something like, ``You can buy whatever you want from the store,`` and used that as her excuse not to prepare anything.
...She's not wrong, but...@k Still... having absolutely nothing there is oddly infuriating...
Humans really are selfish creatures.
``Hurry up and get here already... Mion.``
I muttered to myself.
Why, you ask?@k Because she'd volunteered for this on her own.
changeReason: Old says 'she wouldn't let me say something' (refusal) which is the opposite of JPN 勝手に言い出した (she volunteered/initiated it on her own).

TextENGNew: In today's club activity, Mion came in dead last by a mile.
Naturally... Rena and I tied for first place.
So in the end, after Mion accepted Rena's shopping list, she rounded on me with a look as if she was about to eat me alive.
``At this point, one person or two, it doesn't matter!@k@vS17/03/110300102. So go on, out with it! Tell me what you want me to buy!``
...That was the first time in my life someone had ever threatened me into letting them do me a favor.
changeReason: The original says 'someone threatened to buy me something' but the Japanese means 'someone threatened me into making a request of them' — the direction of coercion is reversed.

TextENGNew: Since I had no choice, I decided to give her 'my own personal errand.'
But the look on Mion's face at that moment... she just tilted her head to the side and said,
``...This?@k@vS17/03/110300104. This is all you want?@k@vS17/03/110300105. And... this is it?``
changeReason: The original translates しかも…これ？ as 'Nothing besides that?' but しかも means 'moreover/and yet' — Mion isn't asking if there's more, she's expressing surprise at what the specific item is.

TextENGNew: She kept asking me over and over to confirm.
changeReason: The original says 'I heard it over and over' but the Japanese describes Mion repeatedly asking for confirmation (聞き直していた) — the subject and action are both wrong in the original.

TextENGNew: Yes, that's fine! I'm starving, so hurry up and buy it for me~!
...Just as I was about to shout that, it happened.
The doorbell rang. I headed to the entrance and opened the door... and there stood Mion.
``Here you go!``
``...Perfect timing.``
``Hmm? ...Kei-chan.@k@vS17/03/110300108. Were you just about to look up at the sky and shout, 'Hurry up and buy it, Mion!' weren't you?``
``H-how did you know?!``
``I knew it~! Aahahahaha!``
``How could you tell, Mion?``
``Because I could hear you, silly.``
``S-seriously?!``
Seeing the look of panic on my face, Mion cackled and said, 'Just kidding~!'@k ...She got me.
``That aside... why did you ask me to get something like this?@k@vS17/03/110300112. And only a single one at that...``
Her eyes were fixed on the one item she'd gone all the way to buy for me.
Cup noodles.@k A specific brand, no less... the mega-sized pork bone and ginger flavor...
``What does it matter?@k@vS17/01/110100099. It's my favorite, and it's going to be my dinner tonight...``
``Whaaat?!@k@vS17/03/110300114. This is your dinner?!``
``Well yeah, my mom's not home today.``
``Oh, is that all...@k@vS17/03/110300116. If that's the case, maybe I should've made you something instead?``
``...You?@k@vS17/01/110100102. You can actually cook, Mion?``
``How rude!@k@vS17/03/110300118. Don't underestimate this old man!@k@vS17/03/110300119. Cooking is my absolute specialty!``
changeReason: The original says 'more than capable' but the Japanese 得意中の得意 means 'the greatest of my strengths' — Mion is boasting about cooking being her top skill, not merely claiming competence.

TextENGNew: Mion planted her hands on her hips and stood there defiantly, radiating indignation.
changeReason: The original says Mion 'mouthed off,' but the Japanese describes a physical stance: 仁王立ち (standing like a temple guardian statue) showing anger — a visual description, not verbal confrontation.

TextENGNew: But... I honestly couldn't believe it. With that face, that outfit? ...No way.
``...What's with that look?``
``I mean, I've never actually seen you cook...``
``Hmph, I'll show you one of these days.@k@vS17/03/110300122. And when I do, you'll be completely blown away!``
``Sure, I'll hold you to that.``
After I answered with a cheerful grin, Mion picked up the bags at her feet.
changeReason: The original attributes the cheerful smile to Mion, but the Japanese structure suggests Keiichi is the one who answered cheerfully (にこやかに答えて), after which Mion picked up her bags.

TextENGNew: Both her hands were weighed down by enormous shopping bags.
...No, seriously, that was an insane amount of stuff... I couldn't help myself and peeked inside without asking.

500 grams of sliced pork, 2 cartons of eggs, 5 blocks of tofu, 4 cartons of milk, 1.8 liters of soy sauce... plus carrots, long onions, pumpkin, onions, potatoes, green beans, napa cabbage, shiitake mushrooms, king oyster mushrooms, komatsuna greens, bean sprouts...
changeReason: The original translates 小松菜 as 'spinach' but it's 'komatsuna' (Japanese mustard greens), a different vegetable; also 豆腐5丁 uses the proper counter '5 blocks' vs the vague '5 servings.'

TextENGNew: ...I did a double take and looked more carefully... there was a whole row of small spice bottles.
Mion pulled out Rena's shopping list from her pocket and started reading off the names of the spices.
``Let's see... for bittering and coloring agents: saffron, paprika, turmeric, and gardenia... right.@k@vS17/03/110300124. And then... for pungent spices: pepper, chili, wasabi, and mustard...``

``And lastly, for aromatic spices... clove, mint, thyme, rosemary,@k@vS17/03/110300126. sage, basil, fennel, caraway, fennel seed,@k@vS17/03/110300127. anise, oregano, watercress, capers, bay leaves, coriander...``
Each time she read a name, I checked it against the label on the little bottle.
And then... at the very bottom of the bag... a whole watermelon!
``Well, Rena did say she wanted to try making authentic cuisine next time~``
...Even so, there are limits! What on earth is she planning to make? Authentic curry? Italian cooking?!
It honestly seems more like harassment than a shopping list...
On top of that... there was another bag with a similarly packed shopping list--this one being Mion's own. The sheer volume was staggering.
``Mion.@k@vS17/01/110100106. You carried all of this here by yourself?``
``Yep.@k@vS17/03/110300130. It's my penalty, after all.``
I looked at Mion's face.@k Then I looked at her arms, straining under the weight.
She wore a composed expression on her face, but the tendons in both arms were standing out sharply, and her arms were trembling ever so slightly.
changeReason: Old adds 'running down her neck' and 'sapping her of all her strength' not in the Japanese, which only describes arm tendons standing out and slight trembling.

TextENGNew: ``Well then, Kei-chan~@k@vS17/03/110300132. See you tomorrow~... hngh.``
Mion turned around and lumbered off down the road she'd come from.
It felt like the very ground was sagging under the weight.
``...Wait, Mion.``
Before I knew it, I'd broken into a run.
``I said wait.``
``...What is it?``
``I've got nothing better to do anyway.@k@vS17/01/110100110. Let me help you carry those.``
The moment I said that, I practically snatched one of the shopping bags from her and started walking briskly ahead.
``It's fine.@k@vS17/03/110300135. No, really, I'm fine... H-hey, hold on...``
I'd expected her to refuse, so I just ignored her protests and kept walking at a clip.
Before long, I heard Mion's footsteps approaching from behind.
When she caught up and fell into step beside me, she flashed a bashful little grin.
``Thanks.``
...Huh?
``Yeah... they were a bit heavy after all~``
She was being unusually honest.
changeReason: The original translates 素直 as 'amicable' (friendly), but 素直 means 'honest/straightforward/candid' — Mion was being unusually open and genuine, not merely friendly.

TextENGNew: Normally, she would have stubbornly refused.@k But it was clear that something about Mion was different today.
It was as though her usual tomboyish bravado had retreated into the shadows.
Two shadows walked side by side down the road as the sun dipped low.@k The cries of the evening cicadas rang out all around us, filling the air.
changeReason: The original says 'sunny road' but the Japanese 陽の落ちた道 means 'the road where the sun had set/dipped' — it's dusk, not a bright sunny scene, which sets a completely different atmosphere.

TextENGNew: ``What's wrong, Mion?``
It wasn't necessarily because of that, but...@k I decided to go ahead and ask.
changeReason: The original says 'It was bugging me' as a straightforward statement, but the Japanese だから、というわけでもないが means 'It wasn't necessarily because of that, but...' — a hedging disclaimer about his motivation for asking.

TextENGNew: ``Hm? What do you mean?``
``Well, you just seem like you're a bit down.``
``...It's nothing.``
That was all Mion said, and we went back to walking in silence.@k ...And then.
``What?``
I'd been bothered by something since earlier that day, so I decided to at least ask about that.
``You got called out during club time, right?@k@vS17/01/110100115. Who came to see you?``
``Oh, that... it was just... you know...``
``...Just... what?``
When she came back, she'd seemed visibly drained.@k Rena had noticed Mion's demeanor too and asked her about it.
It had been bothering me as well.
``...Could it be... something about last night...?``
The instant she heard that, Mion drew a sharp breath and stopped walking.
changeReason: The original says 'took a deep breath' (calm/deliberate), but 息を呑んで means to 'draw a sharp/shocked breath' or gasp — it's a startled reaction, not composed breathing.

TextENGNew: She turned on me sharply, fixing me with her gaze.
changeReason: The original says 'turned to look around,' but キッと振り向き means she turned sharply/piercingly toward Keiichi specifically — it's a confrontational gesture, not looking around.

TextENGNew: ...There it was again. Those eyes.
Those eyes with their unsettling, piercing gleam--the same ones I'd seen on the night of Watanagashi, when she was grilling me about my conversation with Shion...
changeReason: The original says Mion was 'asking Shion,' but the Japanese says Mion was asking KEIICHI about his conversation with Shion (詩音との話の内容をあれこれ訊いてきた) — the interrogated person is different.

TextENGNew: ``...What do you mean... last night...?``
``What about... last night...?``
Pinned by that piercing gaze, I involuntarily flinched.
A wave of tension seized me, as if invisible hands were gripping the back of my neck.
changeReason: The original says 'strangling,' but 首の根元を鷲づかみ specifically describes being grabbed firmly at the base of the neck — a controlling grip, not strangulation.

TextENGNew: When someone finds themselves locked in the gaze of an overwhelmingly powerful adversary, fear can sometimes drive them to act against their own will.
And so, I... my mouth moved on its own.

``I-I mean... I was just wondering if maybe something happened after the festival...@k@vS17/01/110100120. You know... that curse where people die mysterious deaths or suddenly go missing... I heard about it... that's all...``
Perhaps I'd been harboring a quiet frustration, deep down, about the fact that Mion never told me anything.
changeReason: Critical reversal: the original says Mion was feeling frustrated, but the Japanese clearly describes KEIICHI harboring frustration about Mion never telling him anything — the subject of the frustration is wrong.

TextENGNew: In any case, it was undoubtedly a naive and clumsy question.
But Mion's expression was severe.
``Who told you about that?``
``...Who told you...?!``
I couldn't answer right away.
Mion's eyes flew wide open, and at the same moment--
*CRASH!!!*
changeReason: The original uses '*bang*' but グァシャッ is a crashing/shattering sound effect; '*CRASH*' more accurately represents the violent smashing of a grocery bag.

TextENGNew: The shopping bag in Mion's hand was slammed against the ground.
Its contents burst out and scattered across the road.
For an instant, it felt as though everything in the world had stopped.
And then.
A damp, fetid wind swept past, as if mocking us.
changeReason: The original omits あざ笑うように ('as if mocking/sneering'), which personifies the wind and adds an ominous quality to the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Oh... sorry.``
Mion's face registered surprise, as though she'd suddenly come back to herself. She crouched down on the road and began picking up the scattered contents.
``Oh... it's fine...``
I crouched down too, helping Mion gather everything up.
In the midst of it all, Mion murmured quietly, as if confirming something she already knew.
changeReason: The original says Mion 'idly said what she was already certain of,' but the Japanese ぼそっと means 'muttering/mumbling quietly' and 確かめるように means 'as if confirming' — she's checking, not stating with certainty.

TextENGNew: ``...It was Shion, wasn't it.``
I couldn't deny it.
``...I figured.@k@vS17/03/110300153. That girl, sticking her nose where it doesn't belong--``
She spat the words out bitterly.
``M-Mion... Shion didn't really--``
I couldn't find the words to finish.
Suddenly, I caught a fragment of Mion's voice... but it was too quiet to make out.
I turned to look at her. She was still crouched down, picking things up, and I could see her lips moving.
...What? Mion, what are you saying--?
``Stay... away... from Shion.``
Those words carried a crushing, suffocating weight.
In an instant, her face was right in front of mine, and I could clearly see my own reflection in Mion's sharp, piercing eyes.
changeReason: The original omits the detail that Keiichi sees his own reflection (俺の姿) in Mion's eyes, which emphasizes the close proximity and intensity of the confrontation.

TextENGNew: It was as though I'd been swallowed up by a dark, eerie void of pitch blackness... My body refused to move.
``Stay away from Shion. Do you understand?``
Trembling, I nodded.
I... couldn't find words.
...Then, suddenly, something in Mion's eyes seemed to flip back to normal.
And the next instant, she was cackling with laughter.
``...Aahahahaha, Kei-chan!``
``Look at you!``
I finally realized I'd fallen flat on my rear and was sitting in the middle of the road.
...How pathetic.
``You look just like a frog! Aahahahahahaha!``
...A frog?
``A frog flipped on its back with its belly sticking out!``
Gyahahaha! Mion was howling with laughter.
This was the normal Mion... right?
Mion and I gathered up all the spilled groceries, then set off walking toward Rena's house again.
After walking a while, Mion spoke in a quiet voice.
``Kei-chan.@k@vS17/03/110300163. Kei-chan... you're my friend, right?``
With those words, she turned to me with a gentle smile and continued.
``Once things settle down, I'll tell you everything.@k@vS17/03/110300165. I promise.@k@vS17/03/110300166. So... please don't go near Shion anymore.``
``Y-yeah... okay...``
I couldn't bring myself to say anything more.
Seeing that, Mion lowered her gaze, hiding her expression, and murmured softly.
``...Shion's been getting too out of hand.@k@vS17/03/110300168. If she keeps snooping around like that... I suppose it can't be helped...``
------'It can't be helped'......?
Those words alone seemed to creep toward me from Mion's dimly visible silhouette, sending a chill down my spine.
The cries of the evening cicadas... echoed through the air around us once more.
